name: PULSE BIOSCIENCES, INC.
business: Pulse Biosciences, Inc. is a novel ablation company using its patented Nano-pulse Stimulation (NPS) technology to develop and commercialize the nPulse System for treating atrial fibrillation and soft tissue ablation. The company's primary product is the Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System, cleared by the FDA for soft tissue ablation in percutaneous and intraoperative surgical procedures. The company also is developing cardiac surgical clamps and catheters for the treatment of atrial fibrillation.

exposures.sentence: In March 2024, we received FDA 510(k) clearance for our Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System for use in the ablation of soft tissue in percutaneous and intraoperative surgical procedures.

Additionally, we are also pursuing the treatment of atrial fibrillation via a surgical approach as well as select other markets where nsPFA technology could have a profound positive impact on healthcare for both patients and providers, such as surgical soft tissue ablation.
Our nPulse Cardiac Catheter System is uniquely designed to provide a circumferential, or circular, ablation in a single treatment cycle.
Our surgical cardiac ablation clamp is designed for use by cardiac surgeons during the surgical treatment of AF.

catalysts.event: FDA 510(k) clearance for Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System
catalysts.sentence: In March 2024, we received FDA 510(k) clearance for our Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System for use in the ablation of soft tissue in percutaneous and intraoperative surgical procedures.

catalysts.event: FDA 510(k) clearance for second percutaneous electrode needle
catalysts.sentence: More recently, in August 2024, we received FDA 510(k) clearance for a second size of the percutaneous electrode needle, which we believe will provide our customers with an additional treatment option for their patients.

catalysts.event: Breakthrough Device Designation for nsPFA Cardiac Surgery System
catalysts.sentence: In July 2024, we received Breakthrough Device Designation from the FDA for our nsPFA Cardiac Surgery System for the treatment of AF.

catalysts.event: IDE approval for cardiac surgical clamp pivotal trial
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we received approval of our Investigational Device Exemption (IDE) to initiate our pivotal clinical trial of the cardiac surgical clamp and clinicians have already enrolled and treated the first patient in the study.

catalysts.event: Commencement of PRECISE BTN clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: More recently, in September 2025, we commenced a clinical trial (PRECISE BTN) to generate clinical evidence to demonstrate the safety and effectiveness of this less-invasive thyroid preserving procedure and support commercialization of the Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System in the United States.

catalysts.event: First commercial sale of Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System
catalysts.sentence: The total revenue generated was in connection with the limited market release commercial sales of the Vybrance Percutaneous Electrode System with the first commercial sale occurring in the third quarter of 2025.

catalysts.event: FDA IDE approval for cardiac catheter study
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, the FDA approved our IDE submission, and we have begun clinical site qualifications to begin enrollment.

catalysts.event: Presentation of 6- and 12-month follow-up data for cardiac catheter at AF Symposium
catalysts.sentence: More recently, in February 2026, at the AF Symposium, Dr. Reddy presented 6- and 12-month follow up data for the first 150 patients from this study.

tamClaims.claim: Greater than $3.0 billion addressable market in electrophysiology
tamClaims.sentence: This potentially represents a greater than $3.0 billion addressable market within electrophysiology alone combined with long-term double-digit growth.

tamClaims.claim: 1.9 million patients diagnosed with atrial fibrillation annually in the US
tamClaims.sentence: where approximately 1.9 million patients in the United States are diagnosed annually

tamClaims.claim: 43 million people worldwide affected by atrial fibrillation
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that 43 million people worldwide are affected by AF.
